entry.title: Introducing the new "Test Plan Status" filter in Test Executions app in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Running a Focused and Targeted Testing Process

In the world of software development, ensuring that your testing process remains focused and targeted is essential. Unnecessary information like test cases, which are not meant to be tested, shown to the tester can irritate and probably delay the execution. This blog post discusses the new functionality to reduce the test case list in the Test Execution app to only show test cases, which are related to an active test plan.

With this, we build on the already available functionality of using test plans for an advanced test orchestration, which is perfectly described in the related blog post Advanced Test Orchestration and Tester assignment using Test Plans in SAP Cloud ALM. Don’t miss reading it beforehand.

 

Introducing the "Test Plan Status" Filter

To help testers focus on test cases linked to an active test plan, a new filter, “Test Plan Status,” has been added to the Test Execution app. By default, this filter is set to “In Testing,” which does exclude all test cases from the execution list, which are not assigned to a test plan, or which are assigned to a finished test plan.

Along with the new filter, we have moved the “(None)” option in the Test Plan filter to the last position to ensure that customers who exclusively work with Test Plans are not inconvenienced by this entry.

 

How to Use the "Test Plan Status" Filter in Cloud ALM

For those utilizing Cloud ALM for test management, especially if you’re managing test cases only with structured test plans, this feature can be particularly useful. Here’s how you can make the most of it:

  1. Default Filter Setting: By default, the “Test Plan Status” filter is set to “In Testing.” This configuration ensures that testers focus on the cases that require attention without getting sidetracked by those not in progress or which are not associated to a test plan.

  2. Customizing the Filter: If your workflow doesn’t include a formal test plan or if you want a view of all test cases, you can modify the filter. Simply remove the default filter or set it additionally to “(None)” to include all test cases, even those outside the designated test plans.

  3. Saving Your Preferences: Once you adjust the filter settings to your needs, you can save these as a personal view. This setup allows for a customized testing view that aligns with your specific testing requirements and preferences. You can set them also as Default view, which allows a direct filtering for your preferences whenever entering the Test Execution app.

 

Conclusion

A focused testing process can make all the difference in delivering reliable, high-quality software. By leveraging the possibilities of the new “Test Plan Status” filter in Cloud ALM, testers can streamline their efforts and maintain a targeted approach.

entry.title: Q&A for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - How to get your answer faster!
entry.content:
Welcome to the SAP Community!

This is your go-to community for any how-to question on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Our members include experts from customers, partners and SAP who are here to share their knowledge with you and build a strong community together.

Now here are 6 easy steps to help you post a question that will actually be answered faster:

1. Select category “Enterprise Resource Planning”
We recommend the ERP category for your question on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. 
Would you like to post your question in the private Partner Group instead? Go here: Partner Delivery Group

2. Choose the correct managed tag(s)
Did you know that our experts and most community members use tags to find relevant content and track questions? Choosing the correct tag for your question will directly send it to the right experts!

Here’s a list of most used SAP S/4HANA Cloud tags. Please choose the tag that fits your topic or business area best:

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance 
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Sales
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Service
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Manufacturing
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Supply Chain
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Asset Management
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Sourcing and Procurement
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Professional Services
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Human Resources
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Research and Development
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud for enterprise portfolio and project management
  • SAP Project and Resource Management
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Localization
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Extensibility
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Master Data
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Data Migration

If you have a very general question that does not really fit with one of the topic tags, you can use the general tag (but this should be the exception):

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Is your question about another product? Follow this link to find a list of all managed tags in SAP Community.

3. Enter a smart subject
A smart subject line that clearly mentions the exact topic or issue will help experts to find your question faster!

4. Describe your issue as detailed as possible
The more details you can provide, the better we can understand your issue and help you out faster! Use screenshots to show what you are trying to do in the system (but make sure not to disclose any sensitive information captured in the screenshot).
All this will help reduce follow-up questions that can slow down the process.

5. Respond to comments
After you have posted your question, our experts may add a comment to ask for more details. SAP Community will send you a notification that a user has commented on your question. Don't forget to respond in time so that they can help you faster!

6. Accept a helpful answer as solution
Are you happy with the answer that you received? Make sure to click “Accept as Solution” so that other users can see that your problem was solved. You can also give a kudo to show your appreciation.

Ready to get started? Post your first question!

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can find more enablement for SAP S/4HANA Cloud here:

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Community Page
  • Enablement Assets
  • Explore the ready-to-run cloud ERP here: https://www.sap.com/products/erp/s4hana.html
  • Watch our SAP S/4HANA Cloud product update videos on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLWV533hWWvDnnyN2j-CcUheNN-GaNCb3H
  • Check out our digital enablement wheel here: https://chart-bdmaicr0au.dispatcher.eu2.hana.ondemand.com/index.html?hc_reset
  • Listen to our podcast here: https://podcast.opensap.info/inside-sap/
  • Watch our openSAP microlearnings to learn about SAP S/4HANA Cloud topics at your own pace here: https://microlearning.opensap.com/
  • Find best practices on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition here: https://me.sap.com/processnavigator/SolS/EARL_SolS-013/latest?region=DE
  • Check out what’s new here: https://help.sap.com/doc/ce01d82756b947a1a043a5d5a3204226  
  • Find help here: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD
  • Read the feature scope description here: https://help.sap.com/doc/7c9e0bbbd1664c2581b2038a1c7ae4b3

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408.3: Final What's New & Product Assistance available
entry.content:
With immediate effect, the final What's New information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408.3 is now available. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is based on the technical product SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

To access the Final What’s New information, please use the What’s New Viewer.

For further formats and languages of the What's New information, as well as pre-filtered views, please use the following SAP S/4HANA Cloud Help Portal page.

In addition, the final Product Assistance (aka Application Help) for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408.3 is available. Please use the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Help Portal page to access the Product Assistance in multiple languages.

For you as a customers, we recommend to also use the Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool.

The Release Assessment and Scope Dependency (RASD) tool provides you with a personalized and tailored view on What’s New information based on the activated and used scope within your system. The information for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408.3 will be available in RASD within the next few days. A valid Customer S-user ID is required for login. We recommend to use RASD to prepare for upgrades and updates of your system and to focus on the new and changed features that are relevant for you as a customer.

There is a new commenting feature available for What’s New. Authors can now evaluate your feedback and incorporate it into the documentation, if needed. We hope that by opening this channel of communication, we can learn how to further improve our What’s New content for you. 

To use this feature, you need to be logged into the Help Portal. The feature will only be available in the English version of the documents. To learn more about how the feedback process works in SAP Help Portal, see Giving Documentation Feedback.

Both logistic and finance employees suffer a lot due to the function limit.

Improvement Request Details - Customer Influence

entry.title: EDI vs. SAP CPI: Choosing the Best Solution for Supplier Order Confirmations
entry.content:
In today’s fast-moving business world, keeping your supply chain running smoothly is more important than ever. A key part of that is automating supplier order confirmations. However, businesses often ask: Do we need to use EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) to automate these confirmations, or can we convert vendor email confirmations into SAP order confirmations using SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI)?

In this blog, we’re going to break down both options, compare their benefits and challenges, and help you figure out which solution will work best for your business.

1. Introduction: Activating Supplier Order Confirmations in SAP

When you place an order with a supplier, you expect a confirmation to make sure everything is correct and the order is being processed. Traditionally, these confirmations are sent by email, fax, or phone, which can slow things down and lead to mistakes.

To make things easier and faster, companies use SAP to automate and track order confirmations. The big question is: Should you use EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) or SAP CPI (Cloud Platform Integration) to handle the data between your system and your suppliers?  we’ll compare both options and help you choose the best one for your business.

 

2. How EDI Automates Supplier Order Confirmations

EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) is a way for businesses to send documents, like order confirmations, electronically in a standard format. When both you and your supplier use EDI, the process of exchanging order confirmations becomes automatic and much more efficient.

How it works:

  • The supplier sends the order confirmation through EDI, using a format like IDoc, EDIFACT, or X12.
  • Your SAP system gets the EDI message, processes it, and automatically creates an order confirmation in SAP.

Advantages:

  • Fully Automated: Once set up, everything happens automatically, with little to no manual work.
  • Standardized: EDI uses a common format that both systems understand, making it easy to exchange data.
  • Reliable: Since it’s automated, there’s less room for human error, making the process more accurate.

Considerations:

  • Requires Both Systems to Support EDI: Both you and your supplier need to have EDI set up.
  • Setup and Maintenance Costs: EDI can be expensive to set up and maintain, as it requires technical configuration and ongoing support.

In short, EDI is a great way to automate order confirmations, but it does require some upfront investment from both sides to set it up. Once it's running, it works smoothly and reliably.

 

3. Automating Order Confirmations Without EDI: SAP CPI for Email-Based Confirmations

Not every supplier uses EDI. Some prefer to send order confirmations by email, often in formats like PDF, Excel, or Word. This is where SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI) comes in. It lets you automatically process email-based order confirmations into your SAP system—no need for EDI.

How it works:

  • The supplier sends an order confirmation via email (usually as a PDF, Excel, or Word file).
  • SAP CPI reads the email and pulls out the important details like order number, quantities, and delivery info.
  • It then converts this data into a format SAP can use (like IDoc or BAPI) and updates your SAP system with the order confirmation.

Advantages:

  • No EDI Needed: If your supplier doesn’t have EDI, SAP CPI lets you process their email confirmations without extra tech.
  • Flexible: SAP CPI can handle different email formats (PDF, Word, Excel) and turn them into usable data for SAP.
  • Cost-Effective for Small Suppliers: Small suppliers who can’t invest in EDI can still send email confirmations, and you can automate the process using SAP CPI.

Considerations:

  • Complex Email Formats: Parsing unstructured data from PDFs or free-text emails can be tricky and might need a custom setup in SAP CPI.
  • Ongoing Maintenance: If your supplier changes their email format, you may need to adjust the parsing rules in SAP CPI to keep everything working smoothly.

In short, SAP CPI is a great option for automating email-based order confirmations when your supplier doesn’t use EDI. It’s easy to set up and saves time, but it does need a bit of maintenance to handle different email formats.

 

4. Which Solution Is Right for You? EDI vs. SAP CPI

  • EDI is the best choice if both you and your supplier support it. It’s the most efficient, automated, and standardized way to manage order confirmations.
  • SAP CPI is a great option if your supplier doesn’t use EDI or prefers to send order confirmations by email. It’s flexible and can handle different email formats, but it might need more setup and ongoing maintenance.

5. Conclusion: Simplifying Supplier Order Confirmations

If your supplier already uses EDI, sticking with it is probably the best choice. It’s fast, reliable, and fully automated, making it easier to keep everything in sync.

If your supplier prefers to send email confirmations, SAP CPI can handle that too. While it may require some setup and maintenance, it allows you to automate the processing of emails into your SAP system, even if EDI isn’t an option.

Do You Need EDI for Both Systems?

No, you don’t need EDI if you’re using SAP CPI to process email-based confirmations. However, if both parties support EDI, it’s usually the more efficient and standardized choice.

By understanding the benefits and limitations of both options, you can choose the right solution to streamline your order confirmation process. Whether you use EDI or SAP CPI, automating this process will save time, reduce errors, and make your supply chain more efficient.

Hope this blog is helpful! Feel free to reach out if you have any questions or need help setting up either solution!
